title: Torch Clock (3-Torch Ring)
description: Three wall torches in a triangle, each suppressing the next. Because each torch's output powers the base of the next torch, they take turns switching off in sequence — creating a fast oscillating signal. Period is roughly 3 game ticks (1.5 redstone ticks). Unstable on some hardware; prefer a hopper clock or dropper clock for precision timing.

steps.title: Stone base
steps.explanation: Five stone blocks: three at the triangle corners (0,0,0), (2,0,0), (2,0,2) and a fourth corner (0,0,2), plus a connector block (0,0,1) on the west side. The west column is the feedback path that closes the ring.

steps.title: Three wall torches
steps.explanation: Torch_A on the east face of (0,0,0), Torch_B on the south face of (2,0,0), Torch_C on the west face of (2,0,2). Each torch outputs into the next corner stone: A→B, B→C, C→west column. The ring is now live — you should see them flicker.

steps.title: Feedback dust and output tap
steps.explanation: Torch_C's output powers stone (0,0,2). Run dust up the west column: (0,1,2)→(0,1,1)→(0,1,0). The dust at (0,1,0) strongly powers stone (0,0,0), which suppresses Torch_A — closing the ring. Tap your clock signal from any of these three dust positions.
